methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $10.3B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $539M - $3.8B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.9x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: New Limit

data.narrative:
Colossal Biosciences, a Series C-stage biotech company founded by Ben Lamm and George Church, has raised $555M+ and carries a last post-money valuation of $10.3B. The valuation analysis triangulates across a comparable universe of 3 public equities (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private peers in Biotech & Health, including New Limit, Chai Discovery, and Eikon Therapeutics.

Two applicable valuation methods were deployed. Stage-progression analysis applies a typical 1.7x step-up multiplier from Series C to next-stage financing, yielding $17.5B. Peer-median methodology across three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables—New Limit ($3.1B Series C), Chai Discovery ($3.8B Series B), and Eikon Therapeutics ($539M Series B)—produces a $3.1B median valuation, with a range of $539M to $3.8B. Public-comp anchoring via price-to-sales multiples (sector median 6.9x across three tickers) cannot be applied due to absence of target revenue disclosure, typical for private companies.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $2.6B (low) to $20.2B (high), with a median of $10.3B. The substantial spread reflects material uncertainty around the stage-progression multiplier's appropriateness and peer comparability given Colossal's founding pedigree and capital intensity relative to the private peer set. Revenue visibility and path to profitability remain key unknowns.
